QF.id: XC Case Studies and Practical Application_11

question.text: Rajiv, an NRI (Non-Resident Indian), wants to open a PMS account in India. Which of the following additional requirements applies compared to a resident Indian?

answer.text: NRIs cannot invest in PMS under any circumstances
Rajiv must operate through an NRE or NRO account and comply with FEMA/RBI guidelines in addition to SEBI PMS regulations
Rajiv needs a minimum investment of Rs.1 crore instead of Rs.50 lakh
Rajiv must first obtain SEBI approval before investing in PMS

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NRIs can invest in PMS but must operate through NRE/NRO accounts and comply with both SEBI PMS regulations and FEMA/RBI guidelines for remittance and repatriation. The minimum investment remains Rs.50 lakh. **The Trap:** NRIs are NOT barred from PMS. The minimum is not different. No separate SEBI approval is needed. The additional layer is FEMA/RBI compliance for cross-border flows. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A Chapter 17 covers NRI investment processes. Series XXI-A/B cover PMS regulations for different investor categories. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure the PMS provider accepts NRI clients (not all do) and verify which PMS account types (NRE vs NRO) are available — this affects repatriation flexibility and tax implications.
